input.prompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

input.initialPrompt:
My .NET MAUI page shows blank labels even though my ViewModel properties
have values. Here is my XAML:
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml">
    <VerticalStackLayout>
        <Label Text="{Binding Title}" />
        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" />
    </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```
What am I doing wrong?

data.content:
You're missing the **`BindingContext`**. Without it, the `{Binding}` expressions have nothing to resolve against. Set it in code-behind or XAML:

**Option A — Code-behind (constructor):**
```csharp
public MyPage()
{
    InitializeComponent();
    BindingContext = new MyViewModel();
}
```

**Option B — XAML:**
```xml
<ContentPage xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             ...>
    <ContentPage.BindingContext>
        <vm:MyViewModel />
    </ContentPage.BindingContext>
    ...
</ContentPage>
```

Also ensure your ViewModel implements `INotifyPropertyChanged` (or inherits `ObservableObject` from the MVVM Toolkit) so the UI updates when property values change after initial binding.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a common .NET MAUI data binding issue. The labels are blank even though the ViewModel properties have values. This is a classic problem — they're missing the `BindingContext` assignment.

Let me look at the XAML: they have `{Binding Title}` and `{Binding Description}`, but there's no `BindingContext` set anywhere. That's the most likely issue.

There could also be a secondary issue: the ViewModel properties might not implement `INotifyPropertyChanged`, but the user says the properties "have values," so the primary issue is almost certainly the missing `BindingContext`.
